channel.title: Health - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Doctor who helped ship take care of passengers with hantavirus is isolated in Nebraska medical unit - AP News
item.description:
1. Doctor who helped ship take care of passengers with hantavirus is isolated in Nebraska medical unit  AP News
  2. He was on the trip of a lifetime. Now he’s in a biocontainment unit in Nebraska while hantavirus cases rise  CNN
  3. Hantavirus Attacks Patients’ Bodies. This Doctor Tends to Their Minds.  The New York Times
  4. A Scientist’s Close Call with Hantavirus Aboard the M.V. Hondius Cruise  The New Yorker
  5. CDC not requiring hantavirus cruise passengers to isolate at home  The Hill

item.description:

item.title: Psychology says the loneliest part of being 70 isn’t being alone, it’s being in rooms full of people who love you but no longer expect you to have anything to say - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Psychology says the loneliest part of being 70 isn’t being alone, it’s being in rooms full of people who love you but no longer expect you to have anything to say  Yahoo
  2. One reason some adults have no close friends isn't indifference — it's that they've never found someone who could hold the kind of conversation and connection they actually needed  Space Daily
  3. Psychology suggests that adults in their 70s and 80s don’t always feel lonely because no one loves them; they often feel lonely because there aren’t enough people left who remember who they were before they became “Mom,” “Dad,” or “Grandma”  OkDiario
  4. We’re Living Longer But Getting Lonelier. Here’s What Can Help  AOL.com
  5. Psychology suggests people who reach their 60s without close friends aren't necessarily socially broken — some spent years being needed but rarely known  Space Daily
